From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1978 Italian adventure film

| Ms. Stiletto | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |

| Screenplay by | * [Giorgio Cavedon](/wiki/Giorgio_Cavedon "Giorgio Cavedon") * [Mario Amendola](/wiki/Mario_Amendola "Mario Amendola") * Elisabeth Forster[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016129-1) |
| Story by | * Giorgio Cavedon * Mario Amendola * Elisabeth Forster[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016129-1) |
| Based on | *Isabella, duchessa dei diavoli* |
| Starring | [Brigitte Skay](/wiki/Brigitte_Skay "Brigitte Skay") |
| Cinematography | Fausto Zuccoli[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016129-1) |
| Edited by | [Luciano Anconetani](/w/index.php?title=Luciano_Anconetani&action=edit&redlink=1 "Luciano Anconetani (page does not exist)")[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016129-1) |
| Music by | [Sante Romitelli](/w/index.php?title=Sante_Romitelli&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sante Romitelli (page does not exist)") |
| Production companies | * I.N.D.I.E.F. * Cinesecolo * Houwer-Film, Film- und Fernsehproduktion[[2]](#cite_note-filmportal-2) |
| Release date | * 1969 (1969) (Italy) |
| Running time | 93 minutes[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016129-1) |
| Countries | * Italy * West Germany[[2]](#cite_note-filmportal-2) |

***Ms. Stiletto*** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Isabella, duchessa dei diavoli*), is a 1969 historical [adventure film](/wiki/Adventure_film "Adventure film"), directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci"). The setting is the [Kingdom of France](/wiki/Kingdom_of_France "Kingdom of France") in the early 17th century, while [Louis XIII](/wiki/Louis_XIII "Louis XIII") is still underage. An Alsatian baron kills the family of a Duke and claims their [Château](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teau "Château") for himself. The Duke's daughter is rescued by a [gypsy](/wiki/Roma_people "Roma people") [tribal leader](/wiki/Tribal_leader "Tribal leader"). Several years later, the girl has become a [teenager](/wiki/Teenager "Teenager") and seeks [revenge](/wiki/Revenge "Revenge").

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ms._Stiletto&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

Early 17th century, France. Eric von Nutter, a greedy [Alsatian](/wiki/Alsace "Alsace") [baron](/wiki/Baron "Baron") raids [Duke](/wiki/Duke "Duke") de Frissac's family estate Château Salins and kills his entire family except Isabella, his little daughter. Isabella is saved by Melicour, the head of a [gypsy](/wiki/Roma_people "Roma people") tribe nicknamed The Devils.

Years later, Isabella is a beautiful and courageous teenage girl. By chance, she encounters [François de Bassompierre](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_de_Bassompierre "François de Bassompierre") and gets the opportunity to reclaim her title but Château Salins is now possessed by Von Nutter. France is going through political instability under the child king [Louis XIII](/wiki/Louis_XIII "Louis XIII") and needs support from German princes. Therefore, Isabella is barred from possession of Château Salins by the [Louvre](/wiki/Louvre_Palace "Louvre Palace") and a young [viscount](/wiki/Viscount "Viscount"), Gilbert de Villancourt is assigned as a guardian to her. However, following a failed assassination attempt by a minion of Von Nutter, Isabella vows to avenge her family and take Château Salins back.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ms._Stiletto&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Brigitte Skay](/wiki/Brigitte_Skay "Brigitte Skay") as Isabella de Frissac
* [Mimmo Palmara](/wiki/Mimmo_Palmara "Mimmo Palmara") as Baron Eric von Nutter
* [Fred Williams](/wiki/Fred_Williams_(actor) "Fred Williams (actor)") as Viscount Gilbert de Villancourt
* [Mario Novelli](/wiki/Mario_Novelli "Mario Novelli") as Mayer, Von Nutter's captain
* [Enzo Andronico](/wiki/Enzo_Andronico "Enzo Andronico") as Von Nutter's sergeant
* [Giacomo Furia](/wiki/Giacomo_Furia "Giacomo Furia") as Rudolph, the pimp
* [Tino Scotti](/wiki/Tino_Scotti "Tino Scotti") as Melicour
* [Sal Borgese](/wiki/Sal_Borgese "Sal Borgese") as Diego Mendoza
* [Alberto Sorrentino](/wiki/Alberto_Sorrentino "Alberto Sorrentino") as A gypsy
* [Gioia Desideri](/w/index.php?title=Gioia_Desideri&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gioia Desideri (page does not exist)") as Susanne, Von Nutter's wife
* [Loris Gizzi](/wiki/Loris_Gizzi "Loris Gizzi") as [M.Llo Bassompierre](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_de_Bassompierre "François de Bassompierre")
* [Thomas Astan](/wiki/Thomas_Astan "Thomas Astan") as Kurt von Nutter
* [Renato Baldini](/wiki/Renato_Baldini "Renato Baldini") as Gunther

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ms._Stiletto&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Background")]

*Ms. Stiletto* is an adaptation of the *[fumetto](/wiki/Fumetti_(comics) "Fumetti (comics)")* *[Isabella, duchessa dei diavoli](/wiki/Isabella_(comics) "Isabella (comics)")*, a [comic book](/wiki/Comic_book "Comic book") series by [Renzo Barbieri](/wiki/Renzo_Barbieri "Renzo Barbieri"), who created the character of Isabella, and [Giorgio Cavedon](/wiki/Giorgio_Cavedon "Giorgio Cavedon"), who wrote the stories.[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016129-1)[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016130-3) The comic series became popular in Italy and was Italy's first openly erotic comic strip.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016130-3)

The film was part of a minor trend of comic strip adaptations that emphasised mild [sado-masochism](/wiki/Sado-masochism "Sado-masochism") and late 1960s fetish gear, which started with *[Danger: Diabolik](/wiki/Danger:_Diabolik "Danger: Diabolik")* and *[Barbarella](/wiki/Barbarella_(film) "Barbarella (film)")*.[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTENewman2011255-4)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ms._Stiletto&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Release")]

*Ms. Stiletto* was released in Italy in 1969.[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016129-1) The film was released on home video in the United States under the title *Ms. Stiletto* in the mid 1980s.[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016131-5) As of 2016, the film is only available in the United States through a [VHS tape](/wiki/VHS_tape "VHS tape") from the [Force Video](/wiki/Force_Video "Force Video") label.[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016131-5) Italian film historian and critic described this tape as "badly cropped VHS" that is "sold at a very high price among collectors."[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2016131-5)
